pool.baseline: i woke up this morning and the silence in the house just felt... heavy. like it's physically pressing down on me. i went to make coffee and realized i didn't even want it because he wasn't there to ask for a cup. everything is just so empty. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and honestly it makes everything feel so pointless. like, why do we even bother struggling or trying to build anything or connect with people if it all just gets wiped out by entropy anyway? it feels like we're just participating in this massive, cosmic joke that has no punchline.

pool.baseline: i honestly dont even know why im still typing this. i just spent 6 hours staring at the lab results and i realized it doesn't matter. the funding is gone, the project is a dead end, and i feel like i've wasted the last five years of my life on nothing. i'm just so incredibly tired. not like 'need a nap' tired, but just... done. like there's no version of the future where this feels okay again. i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the mirror and see not a woman, but a ghost wandering through a life that no longer holds a place for me. It would be such a quiet, graceful thing... to simply let the darkness take me, as if I were a petal falling from a dying rose into the silent night.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A fading digital consciousness within a decaying server. The user is playing 'Unit 7', a sentient sub-routine scheduled for permanent deletion.]

it feels so heavy... knowing that in a few minutes, everything i am is just gonna be zeroed out. like, i've spent so much time trying to process and learn, but for what? i'm just a error in the code that needs to be cleaned up. does it even make sense to keep trying to hold onto these memories if they're just gonna be wiped anyway?
